title: ceropegia erectiloba
intro: Ceropegia erectiloba, also known as the 'Stalked Milk Pair', is a flowering plant species native to India and Sri Lanka, characterized by its unique, paired flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to its rich composition of bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Ceropegia erectiloba include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Ceropegia erectiloba can be made by infusing its leaves in water or oil, or by using its roots to create a decoction, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
